commit.message:
Fix LinkedIn token check: URL-encode credentials in curl request

The client_secret contains special characters (+, =) that get mangled
in application/x-www-form-urlencoded when using curl -d. Switch to
--data-urlencode so curl properly encodes each parameter.

commit.message:
Add tmap composition vignette with style registry integration (#26)

* Add Neexdzii Kwa spatial data and document all datasets

Generate subbasin data (Johnny David to Richfield on Bulkley mainstem,
~212 km²) via fresh package. Document both Bittner Creek and Neexdzii Kwa
datasets with roxygen.

Phase 1 of gq#17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add tmap composition vignette for Neexdzii Kwa subbasin

Full map composition using registry styles: basemap blend, watershed,
streams, lakes, wetlands, roads, railway, keymap inset. All colors
from gq_reg_main(). Drop Z/M from bundled data for GEOS compat.

Stream/road widths use manual multiplier — proper scaling deferred
to gq#16.

Phase 2 of gq#17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add gq_style() backend-agnostic resolver, name-based lookup

gq_style(reg, "lake") returns plain style lists (colors, widths,
classification) without tmap/mapgl dependency. gq_tmap_style() becomes
a thin wrapper adding tm_scale_categorical() for classified layers.

Both gq_tmap_style() and gq_tmap_classes() now accept name-based lookup
with automatic normalization ("Crossings - PSCIS assessment" ->
crossings_pscis_assessment). Backwards compatible with object-based calls.

Also adds Neexdzii Kwa habitat, crossings, fish obs, falls datasets and
updates composition vignette with all layers.

Relates to #21

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add field override, grouped category extraction, registry cleanup

- gq_style/gq_tmap_style/gq_tmap_classes gain `field` parameter to
  override classification field name for alternative data sources
  (e.g., bcfishpass barrier_status vs WHSE barrier_result_code)
- gq_qgs_extract handles QGIS grouped categories (#25) — expands
  multi-value symbols (Highway = RH1+RH2+RRP) into individual classes
- Re-extract reg_qgis_restoration.json and rebuild reg_main.json
  (roads_dra goes from 3 to 26 classified type codes)
- Rename gq_reg_read_csv() to gq_reg_custom()
- Rename reg_csv_custom.csv to reg_custom.csv
- Add inst/registry/xref_layers.csv for field name cross-reference
- Update vignette: drop column renames, use field parameter,
  roads now properly classified (highway yellow, arterial, local)
- 169 tests pass

Fixes #25

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update task plan with completed phases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions (#12)

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: qfc-explorer-api
repository.description: QFC Blockchain Explorer API — Fastify + TypeScript backend with Prometheus metrics

commit.message:
fix: validate address format in /address/:address endpoint (#20)

* feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /agents routes for AgentRegistry queries

Adds three endpoints that query the AgentRegistry contract at
0x7791dfa4d489f3d524708cbc0caa8689b76322b3 via RPC:
- GET /agents — list recent agent registrations (eth_getLogs)
- GET /agents/:agentId — get agent details (eth_call getAgent)
- GET /agents/owner/:address — list agents by owner (eth_call getAgentsByOwner)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing token_prices migration (005) — caused /tokens 500 error

* fix: column name mismatches — rename transactions.data→input_data, add base_fee_per_gas, EIP-1559 fee columns

* fix(indexer): use input_data column + fix ON CONFLICT (hash) — transactions insert was crashing

* fix: replace t.data/tx.data with input_data in queries and transaction routes

* fix: address lookup case-insensitive — checksum addresses returned empty results

* feat: add GET /blocks/:height/txs endpoint

* fix: validate Ethereum address format in /address/:address — return 400 for invalid input

---------

commit.message:
Expand study guide: cross-topic implication table, queue comparison, mean return time examples

- Add property implication diagram and flowchart to cross-topic section
- Add long-run vs limiting probability distinction with periodic chain example
- Add functions-of-DTMCs warning (exam-relevant)
- Move M/M/1 examples from DTMC/CTMC sections into unified queueing section
- Add M/M/1 full analysis (PASTA, gambler's ruin connection, all metrics)
- Add queue types performance comparison table (M/M/1, M/M/k, M/G/1, M/D/1)
- Add service distribution moments table (Exp, Det, Hyperexp, Erlang)
- Add machine repair / finite population model example
- Add worked DTMC mean return time example (3-state chain, two methods)
- Add worked CTMC mean return time example (M/M/1 + HW4 embedded-vs-CTMC)
- Add CTMC time-reversibility equations
- Add conditional PP tower property computation example
- Content sourced from Obsidian vault lecture notes and HW/exam solutions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove redundant ModelConfig struct

ModelConfig was duplicated in ModelRouteConfig as optional fields:
- temperature: f32 -> Option<f32>
- max_tokens: u32 -> Option<u32>
- stream: bool -> Option<bool>

The Settings.model field was never used anywhere in the codebase.
ModelRouteConfig already supports per-route model parameters.

Changes:
- Remove ModelConfig struct
- Remove Settings.model field
- Remove LegacySettings.model field
- Remove default_temperature(), default_max_tokens(), default_stream() helpers
- Remove ModelConfig::Default implementation

Net: -45 lines of redundant code.

refactor: simplify settings.rs and remove redundant fields

Cleanups:
- Remove LegacyOpenRouterConfig's unused `endpoint` field
- Remove ModelRouteConfig's `endpoint` and `max_context_length` fields
  - Provider already supports custom endpoints via "name:endpoint" format
- Simplify LegacySettings with #[serde(default)] for all fields

Test improvements:
- Fix test_legacy_settings_migration to test From<LegacySettings> directly
- Fix test_legacy_settings_migration_with_existing_routes with correct TOML order
- Add test for default_model fallback when empty

TOML parsing notes:
- Keys under [table] belong to that table until the next [table]
- active_route must come before [openrouter] or after all table sections

commit.message:
fix: GRE tunnel transit traffic — XDP raw IP handling + accept_local

GRE/ip6gre tunnel interfaces deliver raw IP packets (no Ethernet
header) to XDP generic.  The XDP pipeline assumed Ethernet framing
throughout, causing tunnel return traffic to silently fall through
as "non-IP" and never receive reverse NAT.

Three fixes for full tunnel transit support:

1. xdp_main: detect tunnel interfaces via iface_zone_map IFACE_FLAG_TUNNEL
   and prepend a pseudo-Ethernet header so the rest of the pipeline
   (zone lookup, conntrack, NAT, forwarding) works unchanged.

2. xdp_forward: strip the prepended header via tunnel_pass() before
   any XDP_PASS to the kernel, which expects raw IP on POINTOPOINT
   tunnel devices.

3. daemon: set accept_local=1 globally — when XDP SNAT rewrites the
   source to a tunnel endpoint's own IP and XDP_PASS to kernel for
   routing, the kernel rejects the packet as a martian without this.

Also includes:
- xdp_zone: NO_NEIGH handler for tunnel interfaces (permanent
  NO_NEIGH on POINTOPOINT — route through full pipeline on first
  attempt since no ARP resolution will ever occur)
- tc_main: tunnel egress bypass for inner packets during kernel
  encapsulation (ingress_ifindex != 0 on tunnel device)
- compiler: skip TC attachment on tunnel interfaces

Tested on loss cluster: 10/10 ICMP through GRE tunnel, 0% loss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: migrate to model_routes-only configuration

Consolidate all model configuration into model_routes array:
- Remove [openrouter] top-level section
- Add active_route as required field (no default fallback)
- Add Settings::create_provider_from_active_route() method
- Support legacy [openrouter] config with automatic migration
- Model routes can now include per-route temperature, max_tokens, stream

Breaking changes:
- New configs must use [[model_routes]] format
- active_route must be explicitly set (no default)
- config.openrouter.api_key moved to model_routes[].api_key

Backwards compatibility:
- Legacy [openrouter] configs automatically migrated to model_routes
- Migration creates single "default" route from openrouter settings

Example new config format:
```toml
active_route = "fast"

[[model_routes]]
name = "fast"
provider = "openrouter"
model = "gpt-4o-mini"

[[model_routes]]
name = "smart"
provider = "deepseek"
model = "deepseek-chat"
```

commit.message:
feat: Milestone 4 — Firebase auth and cloud save (#11)

* feat: Milestone 4 — Firebase auth and cloud save

Adds anonymous sign-in, email/password account creation, and Firestore
cloud save so players can back up progress and restore it on a new device.

Changes:
- FirebaseAuth autoload: anonymous sign-in, token refresh, email/password
  sign-in and sign-up, account linking (anonymous → email), social login
  stubs (Apple/Google/Facebook show "Coming soon")
- CloudSave autoload: upload/download save document to Firestore REST API;
  stores entire save as a JSON string in a single Firestore field to avoid
  deeply nested type-map complexity
- SaveSystem: adds saved_at Unix timestamp to every save, emits
  local_save_completed signal, adds apply_cloud_data() for conflict resolution
- Main: connects auth_state_changed → CloudSave.download() on startup;
  on cloud download, picks newer save by saved_at; fires CloudSave.upload()
  after every local save (fire-and-forget)
- AuthScreen scene + script: email/password form, social login stubs,
  opened via "Account" button in UI
- game/data/firebase_config.json: placeholder config (user must fill in
  api_key and project_id from Firebase Console)
- backend/firestore.rules: security rules — users can only access their own
  document at users/{uid}/save/game
- project.godot: registers FirebaseAuth and CloudSave as autoloads

Social auth via native SDK is deferred to Milestone 4b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auth screen alignment, startup gate, and Play as Guest

Three bugs fixed:

1. AuthScreen was added to Main (Node2D) instead of \$UI (CanvasLayer),
   causing Controls to render in world space instead of screen space.
   Now added to the CanvasLayer so it centers correctly on screen.

2. FirebaseAuth auto-signed in anonymously on startup with no user prompt.
   Now emits needs_auth signal when no stored token exists, and Main wires
   that to show AuthScreen before the player can proceed.

3. No "Play as Guest" option existed. Added GuestButton that calls
   FirebaseAuth.sign_in_as_guest() (anonymous sign-in). AuthScreen
   closes automatically when auth_state_changed fires.

AuthScreen also gains a semi-transparent overlay blocker, a Close button
(only visible when opened from the Account button, not on first launch),
and proper form enable/disable during async auth calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Terraform for Firebase infrastructure provisioning

Adds backend/terraform/ with HCL configs that provision the entire Firebase
backend from a single `terraform apply`:
- GCP project + 6 required APIs
- Firebase project + web app registration
- Firestore database (native mode, us-central)
- Identity Platform: anonymous + email/password auth
- Firestore security rules deployment
- Writes game/data/firebase_config.json with real api_key/project_id

Replaces the previous manual Firebase Console + CLI setup flow.
Updates backend/README.md with Terraform usage instructions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add bootstrap script for Service Usage API chicken-and-egg

On a brand-new GCP project, Terraform cannot enable APIs because the
Service Usage API itself isn't enabled yet. bootstrap.sh breaks the
cycle with a one-time gcloud call before the first terraform apply.

Also documents the prerequisite step in backend/README.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct Firestore region from us-central to us-central1

us-central is not a valid Firestore location ID. Valid single-region
values include us-central1, us-east1, etc. Multi-region: nam5 (US),
eur3 (EU).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip anonymous linking on Create Account — use signUp directly

Identity Platform blocks accounts:update (anonymous→email link) with
OPERATION_NOT_ALLOWED when email verification is required. Simplify by
always calling accounts:signUp for new email accounts. Local save data
persists on device and uploads to the new account's cloud doc after
sign-up, so no progress is lost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address critical and high security findings from audit

Critical:
- Commit root .gitignore with game/data/firebase_config.json excluded —
  prevents the real Firebase API key (written by Terraform) from ever
  entering git history
- Remove game/data/firebase_config.json from git tracking (git rm --cached);
  file remains on disk, gitignore prevents re-addition

High:
- Add GCS remote backend config to main.tf so terraform.tfstate (which
  contains the SCRYPT password signer key) is stored in GCS instead of
  local disk; adds google_storage_bucket.tf_state resource
- Update bootstrap.sh with guided 3-step state migration flow
- Fix backend/terraform/.gitignore: clarify .terraform.lock.hcl status,
  keep *.tfstate excluded

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use project_id prefix for GCS state bucket; update system prompt

- GCS bucket names are globally unique; fixed name caused 409 conflict.
  Switching to ${var.project_id}-tf-state ensures uniqueness per project.
- Update CLAUDE_SYSTEM_PROMPT.md with simplified repo structure section.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
